channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Bondi faces a skeptical reception from Democrats at confirmation hearing over her loyalty to Trump
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Pam Bondi, Donald Trump's pick for attorney general, will face sharp questioning from Democratic senators at a confirmation hearing Wednesday expected to center on concerns that the Republican president-elect will look to use the Justice Department's powers to seek retribution agai
item.dc:creator: ERIC TUCKER, ALANNA DURKIN RICHER and MARY CLARE JALONICK, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: Music streams hit nearly 5 trillion in 2024. Women pop performers lead the charge in the US
item.description: More music, more listeners, no problems. The global music industry hit 4.8 trillion streams in 2024, a new single-year record, Luminate’s 2024 Year-End Report found. That’s up 14% from 2023, which held the previous record.
item.dc:creator: MARIA SHERMAN, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: FDA floats plan to make cigarettes nonaddictive, but its fate rests with Trump
item.description: The Biden administration is floating a long-awaited proposal to make cigarettes less addictive by capping their nicotine levels.
item.dc:creator: Matthew Perrone, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Man avoids prosecution for fighting back against migrant subway attack, fatally stabbing 1
item.description: A man who was asleep on a New York City subway train when a group tried to rob him will not face charges after he fatally stabbed one of them.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: US must not become complacent to a growing terrorism threat, a Counterterrorism Center official says
item.description: McLEAN, Va. (AP) — Brett Holmgren got woken up early on New Year's Day by alerts that a driver had plowed into a crowd of revelers in New Orleans. The rampage, which killed 14 people, was the deadliest attack on U.S. soil in years and was inspired by the Islamic State group.
item.dc:creator: ERIC TUCKER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Michelle Obama signals she’s done with Trump
item.description: Michelle Obama is done with President-elect Donald Trump, if her decision to skip next week’s inauguration means anything.
item.dc:creator: Amie Parnes, KREX 5

item.title: 2 private lunar landers head toward the moon in a roundabout journey
item.description: CAPE CANAVERAL, Fla. (AP) — In a two-for-one moonshot, SpaceX launched a pair of lunar landers Wednesday for U.S. and Japanese companies looking to jumpstart business on Earth’s dusty sidekick.
item.dc:creator: MARCIA DUNN, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: The long struggle to establish Martin Luther King Jr. Day
item.description: The Rev. Martin Luther King Jr. gave his iconic “I Have a Dream” speech in 1963 on the steps of the Lincoln Memorial. He chose that location in part to honor President Abraham Lincoln as “a great American, in whose symbolic shadow we stand today.” Now, millions of people honor King in the same way.
item.dc:creator: TERRY TANG, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Fire-scarred Los Angeles is under another rare warning as winds pick up
item.description: Millions of Southern Californians are on edge as winds began picking up during a final round of dangerous fire weather forecast for the region.
item.dc:creator: Jaimie Ding And Julie Watson And John Seewer, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: USDA documented insects and slime at Boar's Head plants, records show
item.description: Government inspectors documented unsanitary conditions at several Boar's Head deli meat plants, not just the factory that was shut down last year after a deadly outbreak of listeria poisoning, federal records show.
item.dc:creator: JONEL ALECCIA, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Cardi B calls for investigation at federal prison in West Virginia
item.description: "THEY ALL KNOW SHES CONNECTED TO ME AND I FEEL THEY DOING THAT TO BE SPITEFUL," Cardi B 's post said.
item.dc:creator: Sam Kirk, NewsNation

item.title: Frantic 911 call captures moments after toddler's freak sledding accident
item.description: Audio from 911 calls captured the frantic moments following a toddler's sledding accident that plunged him into a freezing pond in North Carolina.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Florida bill would ban students in the US without legal permission from some public universities
item.description: TALLAHASSEE, Fla. (AP) — A Florida state lawmaker has filed a bill that would ban some public colleges and universities from admitting immigrants who are in the country without legal permission. The proposal comes the day after Gov.
item.dc:creator: KATE PAYNE, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Missing persons: New Ohio task force to reunite families with loved ones
item.description: Ohio is taking a significant step to change how missing persons cases are handled. Governor Mike DeWine has announced the formation of a new task force designed to improve investigations and reunite families with their loved ones.
item.dc:creator: Brad Hamilton, NewsNation

item.title: Massachusetts superintendent resigns due to 'public mistreatment' after cancer diagnosis
item.description: Alan Strauss was diagnosed with chronic lymphocytic leukemia nearly a year after being named superintendent.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Brill, NewsNation

item.title: Montréal Canadiens hockey player struck by car while in Salt Lake City
item.description: The team later confirmed Emil Heineman suffered an "upper-body" injury and is expected to be sidelined for three to four weeks.
item.dc:creator: Derick Fox, NewsNation

item.title: USDA documented insects and slime at Boar's Head plants, records show
item.description: Government inspectors documented unsanitary conditions at several Boar's Head deli meat plants, not just the factory that was shut down last year after a deadly outbreak of listeria poisoning, federal records show.
item.dc:creator: JONEL ALECCIA AP Health Writer, WFLA, NBC

item.title: House Republicans pass bill to ban transgender athletes from girls’ sports
item.description: House Republicans passed a bill Tuesday to bar transgender student-athletes from competing in girls’ sports, putting the contentious issue front and center following an election cycle that saw it become a frequent target of Republicans.
item.dc:creator: Brooke Migdon, NewsNation

item.title: Biden to lift Cuba terrorism designation, reversing Trump decision in final week in office
item.description: The Biden administration announced on Tuesday that it will remove Cuba from the list of State Sponsors of Terrorism (SST) in exchange for the release of political prisoners jailed for protesting against the regime in July 2021.
item.dc:creator: Laura Kelly, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Historic hot streak: 2024's record heat has NASA seeing red
item.description: An image released by NASA illustrates record global temperatures in 2024 as the average went up by 2.3 degrees Fahrenheit (1.28 degrees Celsius).
item.dc:creator: Greg Haas, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Hegseth grilled over comments on women serving in combat
item.description: The start to hearings for President-elect Donald Trump’s cabinet nominees kicked off on Tuesday with Pete Hegseth who is hoping to become the next secretary of defense.
item.dc:creator: Jordan Connell, WGN 9

item.title: READ: Special counsel's final report in Trump Jan. 6 case
item.description: The Justice Department released special counsel Jack Smith's report on President-elect Donald Trump's election interference case, just days before he returns to the White House.
item.dc:creator: The Hill, ABC 4

item.title: Investigators search landfill site for Michigan mother who vanished 10 days ago
item.description: Police looking for missing Michigan mother-of-two Ashley Elkins are searching through a landfill for evidence in relation to her disappearance. Elkins has not been seen since Jan. 2.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Firefighters deploy, more power is shut off ahead of fierce Southern California winds
item.description: A week after the first fires began, the flames have destroyed thousands of homes and killed at least 24 people.
item.dc:creator: Lily Dallow, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Schools brace for clash with immigration officials ahead of Trump term
item.description: K-12 schools across the U.S. are gearing up for potential efforts to shield their students as the incoming Trump administration sets its sights on deporting millions of people.
item.dc:creator: Lexi Lonas Cochran, WGN 9

item.title: Historic Powerball winner reportedly loses Malibu home in Palisades Fire
item.description: After winning $2.04 billion on a Powerball ticket in 2022, Edwin Castro reportedly bought multiple Los Angeles-area homes.
item.dc:creator: Christine Samra, WGN 9

item.title: Hanging out at Starbucks? It will cost you as company reverses its open-door policy
item.description: (AP) — If you want to hang out or use the restroom at Starbucks, you’re going to have to buy something. Starbucks on Monday said it was reversing a policy that invited everyone into its stores.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: Man accused of sending Caitlin Clark violent messages on X: court docs
item.description: A Texas man is accused of sending sexually violent messages from his X account to Indiana Fever star Caitlin Clark and then driving to Indianapolis, court documents reveal.
item.dc:creator: Matt Christy, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Trump flies US flag at full height before end of 30-day mourning period following Carter's death
item.description: WEST PALM BEACH, Fla. (AP) — U.S. flags at President-elect Donald Trump 's private Mar-a-Lago club are back to flying at full height. Flags are supposed to fly at half-staff through the end of January out of respect for former President Jimmy Carter, who died on Dec. 29.
item.dc:creator: DARLENE SUPERVILLE, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Former Disney World employee to plead guilty to hacking menus, changing allergen information: court records
item.description: A former Walt Disney World employee will plead guilty to hacking menu-creation software to include profanity and alter allergy information, court records indicate.
item.dc:creator: Rachel Tucker, Fox 5

item.title: Photos: Handful of L.A. homes remain untouched amid charred neighborhoods
item.description: The Walsh House, featured in the “Beverly Hills 90210" television series, survived. So did its pristine lawn. Next door, the remains of a neighbor's home was blackened with soot.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Elon Musk donates Cybertrucks to provide LA community with power, Internet amidst wildfire devastation
item.description: A Los Angeles County community near the Eaton Fire is getting help as wildfires have ravaged through residents' homes throughout the past week. Altadena, a census-designated place just north of Pasadena, has experienced power outages as among the many issues wildfires in the area have caused.
item.dc:creator: Aaron Tolentino, Fox 5

item.title: Taquitos sold at Aldi recalled after customer complains of 'dental injury': USDA
item.description: The recall specifically concerns 20-ounce packages of Casa Mamita Chicken & Cheese Taquitos produced by Bestway Sandwiches, Inc.
item.dc:creator: Michael Bartiromo, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Hurts tosses 2 TD passes and Goedert throws 3 stiff-arms to lead Eagles past Packers 22-10
item.description: PHILADELPHIA (AP) — Dallas Goedert fired one stiff arm to a Packers defender, smacked him again and then — in a sports city known for its Broad Street Bullies — dispatched one final shot to the face on his way to end zone.
item.dc:creator: DAN GELSTON, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Clippers, Lakers to resume their wildfire-delayed schedules on Monday
item.description: NBA games are scheduled to return to wildfire-ravaged Los Angeles on Monday night, with the Clippers planning to resume their schedule when they host the Miami Heat and the Lakers set to host Victor Wembanyama and the San Antonio Spurs.
item.dc:creator: BETH HARRIS, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Hurricane Katrina victim forced to evacuate again, this time for Los Angeles wildfires
item.description: The California wildfires are hitting too close to home for a woman who used to live in New Orleans. She was forced to evacuate during Hurricane Katrina, and now she's evacuating once again.
item.dc:creator: Kenny Lopez, Fox 5
